{{nihongo|'''Matsura no Kuni no Miyatsuko'''|末羅国造|''matsura no kuni no miyatsuko'', ''matsura kokusō''}} was a [[kuni no miyatsuko]] who, in [[ancient times]], ruled {{ill|Matsura Province|ja|末羅国}} (covering present-day {{ill|Matsuura|ja|松浦}} in [[Nagasaki Prefecture]], as well as [[Karatsu, Saga|Karatsu]], [[Imari, Saga|Imari]], and the Higashimatsuura District in [[Saga Prefecture]]). The clan is said to have shared a common ancestor with the {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}}.

== Overview ==
According to the {{ill|Kokuzō Hongi|ja|国造本紀}}, during the reign of [[Emperor Seimu]], {{ill|Yata-no-Inagi|ja|矢田稲吉}} (''yata-no-inagi''), grandson of {{ill|Ōmikuchi-no-Sukune|ja|大水口宿禰|lt=Ōmikuchi-no-Sukune}} and of the same ancestral line as the Hozumi-no-Omi, was appointed [[kuni no miyatsuko]], marking the origin of the line.<ref>''Kokuzōsei no Kenkyū: Shiryō-hen, Ronkō-hen'', Yagi Shoten, 2013, p. 280.</ref> Matsura Province is thought to correspond to "Matsuro-koku" recorded in the {{ill|Wajinden|ja|魏志倭人伝|lt=Gishi Wajinden}}, and the [[Kojiki]], {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}, and other sources record names such as Matsura-agata and Matsura no Kuni no Miyatsuko. There is also a theory identifying Yata-no-Inagi with Ōyatako (ancestor of the {{ill|Kusakabe clan|ja|日下部氏|lt=Kusakabe-no-Kimi}}), who according to the {{ill|Hizen no Kuni Fudoki|ja|肥前風土記}} subdued the {{ill|tsuchigumo|ja|土蜘蛛}} of the Karatsu region.

== Notes ==
{{脚注ヘルプ}}
{{reflist}}

== See also ==
* {{ill|Matsura Province|ja|末羅国}}
* {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}}

{{DEFAULTSORT:まつらのくにのみやつこ}}
[[Category:長崎県の歴史]]
[[Category:佐賀県の歴史]]
[[Category:穂積氏]]
{{Japanese-history-stub}}
[[Category:Kuni no miyatsuko]]
[[ja:末羅国造]]
